response.justification: Wei Chen brings genuine geospatial domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map, plus proven low-latency systems work handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby. However, the core requirement of modern C++ (or Rust) is weakly evidenced: his C/C++ work dates to 2014-2015, and his systems-level experience is primarily in DLang, which is adjacent but not equivalent. His recent roles skew toward full-stack TypeScript/Node web development rather than data pipelines or navigation systems, making him a plausible but risky fit worth a technical screen to assess C++ depth.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G speaker)
Low-latency distributed systems work with async I/O and caching at millions of requests/second
Strong engineering culture background: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship
concerns: Modern C++ experience is dated (2014-2015) and brief; primary systems language is DLang
No direct experience with routing engines, map tiling, or navigation data pipelines
Recent roles (2020-present) are web/full-stack focused, away from systems programming
